sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When running NVFP4 Qwen3.5-A35B with SGLang, I noticed that many linear_attn_in_proj layers were not found in the log, which means these layers were quantized. However, the computation precision would be damaged if these layers were quantized. This issue can be observed by running sglang.

sglang.title: [HiCache][SPEC] fix: empty key after page alignment in match_prefix
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

When the input key is shorter than page_size, page_aligned() truncates it to empty. The previous code only checked len(key) == 0 before page alignment, so an empty post-alignment key would proceed into _match_prefix_helper and cause index errors.
Move page_aligned() before the empty-key gua

sglang.title: [HiCache] feat: add draft KV cache backing for L2/L3
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
see #16964
Root Cause
In speculative decoding, the draft and target models share a req_to_token_pool (KV cache index mapping) but maintain separate KV cache pools.
When both HiCache and speculative decoding are enabled, the target KV cache may be loaded back from L2/L3. The shared index t

Root cause: full (max_num_batched_tokens, hidden) buffers were passed into the @support_torch_compile-decorated LLM model, causing inductor to generate kernels that process all 8192 rows during every decode step instead of the actual batch size
Symptom: 100% SM utilization during single-sequence dec

Because numa_bind_nodes is a hashed factor in ParallelConfig.compute_hash, ranks on different NUMA nodes then pr

vllm.title: [Core] Account for `num_gpu_blocks_override` in `max_model_len` checks

Currently, the validation and auto-setting of max_model_len don't take into account num_gpu_blocks_override. This should take precedence over the measured available kv cache size since it's what matters for scheduling purposes.
Without this change, a request that is within max_model_len but does not

Purpose
Fix gemm_a4w4 call in quark_ocp_mx.py to use the updated AITER API signature.
AITER changed gemm_a4w4 to remove the preallocated output tensor parameter and return the output instead.
Old call:
gemm_a4w4(A, B, A_scale, B_scale, out, bpreshuffle=True)

New API:
y = gemm_a4w4(A, B, A_scale, B_

vllm.title: [CI] Return HTTP 400 for unsupported chat content part type
vllm.author: haosdent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
{"type": "file"} (a member of the OpenAI SDK's ChatCompletionContentPartParam union) currently returns 501 because _parse_chat_message_content_part raises NotImplementedError for unrecognized types. Bad user input is 400, not 501.
Switch to VLLMValidationError — mapped to 400 by create_error

re-enabling Flex Attention as an accepted backend for batch invariant mode. to fix the IMA issue, we need to use the strides of the persistent buffer instead of the current tensor so that the memory layout matches what was captured by the cuda graphs.

vllm.title: [QeRL] Add warnings for extra memory buffering
vllm.author: kylesayrs
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Background
Layerwise reloading allows users to incrementally load and process weights as they are loaded into the model. This system relies on buffering layer weights on device until all weights of a layer have been loaded. However, without CPU offloading, this approach necessarily causes excessive

The cohere_command3 tool & reasoning parsers can be used with the command-a-reasoning-08-2025. The cohere_command4 parsers will be used for upcoming models.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix max_num_batched_token not captured in cuda graph
vllm.author: wzhao18
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Currently, cuda graph capture sizes only include batch size that land on multiples of 8 or 16. When max_num_batched_tokens is not a multiple of the stride, it won't be captured. So when the model runs at max batch size, it falls back to eager mode, causing significant performance degradation

vllm.title: [KV Offload] Per-job store completion for CPU offloading connector
vllm.author: Etelis
vllm.bodyExcerpt: When GPU→CPU KV cache stores complete, the scheduler currently only learns about it when the entire request finishes generating (EOS). Blocks that are physically on CPU remain invisible (ref_cnt=-1) for many steps. Future requests sharing the same prefix cannot reuse those blocks and must recompute.
